seo.title: 25 Best Things To Do in Koh Samui (2026 Guide)
seo.metaDescription: Discover the 25 best things to do in Koh Samui in 2026. Temples, waterfalls, island tours, snorkeling, nightlife, and local food. With prices and insider tips.

intro: Koh Samui is far more than beach lounging — though it does that exceptionally well too. Thailand's Gulf island delivers jungle waterfalls, sacred temples, a 42-island marine park, muay thai camps, southern Thai seafood feasts, and easy access to the legendary Full Moon Party on neighboring Koh Phangan. Whether you're kayaking through limestone caves at Ang Thong or eating curried crab at Fisherman's Village, here are 25 things worth doing.

activities.name: Day Trip to Ang Thong Marine National Park
activities.description: Take a speedboat or catamaran to this stunning archipelago of 42 limestone islands. Kayak through emerald lagoons, hike to the Pha Jun Jaras viewpoint for panoramic island views, snorkel over coral reefs, and swim in the famous Thale Nai — a hidden saltwater lake inside Mae Koh island. Full-day tours include lunch and equipment.
activities.price: 1,800–3,500 THB (~$50–100 USD) including park fee and lunch
tips: Speedboat tours give you more time on the islands vs. slow boat
Park may close November–December due to monsoon — check ahead
Bring reef-safe sunscreen and water shoes

activities.name: Visit Big Buddha Temple (Wat Phra Yai)
activities.description: Climb the naga staircase to Koh Samui's most iconic landmark — a gleaming 12-meter golden Buddha statue on a small island connected by a causeway. The hilltop temple offers views across the northeast coast and is surrounded by smaller shrines, bells, and a market selling souvenirs and local snacks.
tips: Dress modestly — free sarongs available at the entrance
Visit early morning (7–9 AM) to avoid tour bus crowds

activities.name: Explore Fisherman's Village & Friday Night Market
activities.description: Wander through Bophut's charming waterfront strip of converted Chinese-Thai shophouses housing boutique shops, galleries, and restaurants. On Friday nights (5–11 PM), the village transforms into Samui's best night market with street food stalls, live music, fire dancers, and handmade crafts.
tips: Friday market is the highlight — arrive by 6 PM for the full experience
Great sunset dining at the beachfront restaurants
activities.bestTime: Friday evening for the night market

activities.name: Swim at Na Muang Waterfalls
activities.description: Visit two jungle waterfalls in the island's mountainous interior. Na Muang 1 drops 18 meters into a large natural swimming pool — an easy walk from the car park. Na Muang 2 is a more impressive 80-meter cascade reached via a 30-minute jungle hike through tropical vegetation.
tips: Wear proper shoes for the Na Muang 2 hike — the trail is rocky and can be slippery
Best water flow after rains (October–January)
Avoid the elephant trekking operators near the parking lot — choose ethical sanctuaries
activities.bestTime: After rain for best water flow

activities.name: Snorkel at Koh Tao (Day Trip)
activities.description: Take a high-speed catamaran to Koh Tao, Thailand's premier dive island. Even without a dive cert, the snorkeling is world-class — crystal-clear waters with reef sharks, sea turtles, and colorful coral at sites like Japanese Garden and Mango Bay. Lomprayah catamarans make the trip in about 2 hours each way.
activities.price: 2,500–4,000 THB (~$70–112 USD) including snorkel gear and lunch
tips: Book the earliest departure to maximize time on Koh Tao
If prone to seasickness, take medication before — the crossing can be rough

activities.name: Visit Wat Plai Laem
activities.description: Explore this colorful lakeside temple complex featuring a dramatic 18-armed Guanyin statue rising from the water and a large laughing Buddha. The temple sits on stilts over fish-filled ponds where visitors can buy food to feed the fish (20 THB). A beautiful fusion of Thai and Chinese Buddhist architecture.
tips: Combine with Big Buddha — only 5 minutes away
Fish feeding is fun for kids
Open 7 AM – 6 PM daily

activities.name: Hike to Secret Buddha Garden
activities.description: Journey to the top of Pom Mountain (460 meters) to discover a hidden sculpture garden created in 1976 by retired fruit farmer Nim Thongsuk. Stone statues of deities, animals, and meditating figures are scattered through natural jungle with streams and small waterfalls. The 4WD ride up the steep dirt road is an adventure itself.
tips: Only accessible by 4WD — the final 2 km is extremely steep and unpaved
Book a safari tour that combines this with Na Muang Waterfalls and viewpoints
Bring water and mosquito repellent

activities.name: Beach Day at Chaweng Beach
activities.description: Spend a day on Samui's longest and most popular beach — 7 km of soft white sand with turquoise waters. Rent a sunbed, try jet skiing or parasailing, or walk to quieter Chaweng Noi at the southern end. Beach bars line the sand with cocktails and Thai food served right to your lounger.
activities.price: Free (sunbed 100–200 THB, water sports from 500 THB)
tips: North Chaweng is calmer; central has the most action
Sunbed is often free if you order food/drinks at a beach bar
activities.bestTime: December to March for calmest seas

activities.name: See Hin Ta & Hin Yai (Grandmother & Grandfather Rocks)
activities.description: Visit Samui's most-photographed natural curiosity — naturally-shaped rock formations on the rocky coast south of Lamai Beach that resemble male and female anatomy. A short boardwalk leads to viewpoints, with food stalls and souvenir shops clustered around the entrance.
tips: Quick visit — combine with a Lamai Beach day
Best photos in morning light

activities.name: Full Moon Party on Koh Phangan
activities.description: Take the 30-minute ferry to neighboring Koh Phangan for the legendary Full Moon Party on Haad Rin Beach. Up to 30,000 revelers dance on the sand under the full moon with fire shows, UV body paint, and dozens of sound systems playing everything from techno to reggae. Runs monthly on the full moon night.
tips: Wear shoes you don't mind ruining — broken glass on the beach is an issue
Don't bring valuables — theft is common in the crowd
Late ferries run until 4–5 AM on party nights from Haad Rin to Samui

activities.name: Visit Wat Khunaram (Mummified Monk)
activities.description: See the remarkably preserved mummified body of Luang Pho Daeng, a revered monk who died in 1973 while meditating. His body is displayed in a glass case wearing sunglasses at this peaceful temple compound in central Samui. A surreal but fascinating cultural experience that offers insight into Buddhist beliefs about meditation and the afterlife.
tips: Combine with Na Muang Waterfalls, which are nearby
Open 9 AM – 5 PM daily
Photography is allowed but be respectful

activities.name: Thai Cooking Class
activities.description: Learn to prepare authentic Thai dishes at one of Samui's many cooking schools. Most classes begin with a market tour to select ingredients, followed by hands-on preparation of 3–5 dishes including tom yum, green curry, pad thai, and mango sticky rice. You eat everything you cook.
tips: Morning classes include a local market visit — more interesting than afternoon-only sessions
Ask about vegetarian/vegan options when booking

activities.name: Lamai Beach & Viewpoint
activities.description: Samui's second-most popular beach offers a more relaxed vibe than Chaweng with good swimming, beach bars, and scenic rocky headlands. Walk south to the Lamai Viewpoint for panoramic coastal views. The area has a laid-back feel with cheaper hotels and restaurants than Chaweng.
tips: Less crowded than Chaweng with a more local feel
Good snorkeling at the rocky northern end of the beach

activities.name: Sunset Cruise
activities.description: Board a traditional Thai longtail boat or luxury catamaran for a sunset cruise along Samui's west coast. Most tours include stops for swimming and snorkeling, a BBQ dinner on board, and open bar as the sun sets over the Gulf of Thailand with Ang Thong islands silhouetted on the horizon.
tips: Catamaran tours are more comfortable and include better food
Book for Friday to combine with a Fisherman's Village market visit after

activities.name: Thai Boxing (Muay Thai) Class
activities.description: Train at one of Samui's many muay thai camps for a single session or multi-day course. Camps like Lamai Muay Thai and Phetchbuncha Stadium offer drop-in classes where you learn basic techniques, pad work, and conditioning. Watch a live fight night at Chaweng Stadium (twice weekly) for the full experience.
activities.price: 500–800 THB per session (~$14–22 USD); fight night tickets 1,500–2,000 THB
tips: Beginners welcome at most camps — no prior experience needed
Bring your own shorts and hand wraps, or buy them at the camp
Fight nights at Chaweng Stadium are on Wednesday and Saturday

activities.name: Overlap Stone Viewpoint
activities.description: A dramatic viewpoint featuring a massive boulder balanced on a hillside overlooking Lamai Bay and the southern coast. The rocky terrain offers multiple vantage points at different heights — the highest provides a 270-degree panorama. A popular spot for sunrise and sunset photos.
tips: Wear proper shoes — the rocks can be slippery
Sunset views here are spectacular

activities.name: Snorkeling at Coral Cove Beach
activities.description: A small, sheltered cove between Chaweng and Lamai with some of the best shore snorkeling on Koh Samui. The rocky headlands on either side are home to colorful reef fish, sea urchins, and the occasional sea turtle. The beach itself is tiny but scenic, backed by boulders and palm trees.
tips: Bring your own snorkel gear for the best fit
Calm conditions January–April offer best visibility
The adjacent Samui Aquarium is worth a look for families
activities.bestTime: January to April for calm, clear water

activities.name: Chaweng Nightlife & Walking Street
activities.description: Experience Samui's liveliest nightlife strip — Chaweng Walking Street (Soi Green Mango). Bar-hop through cocktail lounges, live music venues, go-go bars, and beach clubs. The famous Green Mango nightclub is the island's biggest dance venue. The strip comes alive around 10 PM and runs until late.
activities.price: Free entry to most bars; drinks from 100 THB
tips: Green Mango is the biggest club, but the smaller bars are often more fun
Ladyboy cabaret shows at Starz Cabaret (800 THB) are a popular alternative
Thursday and Saturday are the busiest nights

activities.name: Visit Koh Phangan (Day Trip)
activities.description: Take a 30-minute ferry to Koh Phangan — Samui's party-loving neighbor is also home to pristine beaches, yoga retreats, and waterfalls. Beyond Full Moon, explore Bottle Beach (accessible only by boat or jungle hike), Thong Nai Pan bay, and the Than Sadet waterfall said to have been visited by Thai kings.
tips: Lomprayah and Seatran ferries depart from Nathon and Maenam piers
Rent a scooter on Koh Phangan for the day (200 THB) to explore beyond Haad Rin

activities.name: Spa & Thai Massage
activities.description: Koh Samui is a wellness destination with everything from roadside 300 THB massages to world-class resort spas. Tamarind Springs is famous for its jungle boulder setting, while luxury resorts like Banyan Tree and Six Senses offer elaborate treatment menus. Traditional Thai massage combines acupressure and yoga-like stretches.
tips: Beachside massage at Lamai or Maenam is the most atmospheric budget option
Tamarind Springs requires advance booking — their hot and cold plunge ritual is unmissable

activities.name: Southern Thai Seafood Feast
activities.description: Sample Koh Samui's distinctive southern Thai cuisine — spicier and more intense than central Thai food. Try curried crab with betel leaves, wai khua (coconut curry with octopus), yellow curry with local grouper, and stuffed squid fried in squid ink. Night markets and seafood restaurants at Nathon and Lamai offer the most authentic local food.
tips: Nathon night market has the most authentic and cheapest local seafood
Ask for 'pet nit noi' (a little spicy) if you can't handle full southern Thai heat

activities.name: Kayaking at Ang Thong or Coastline
activities.description: Paddle a kayak through sea caves, mangroves, and hidden lagoons. While Ang Thong Marine Park offers the most dramatic kayaking in the region, several operators also run half-day coastal kayaking trips around Samui's northern or southern headlands with stops for snorkeling and beach picnics.
tips: Early morning sessions avoid the afternoon heat
Coastal trips are a good alternative if Ang Thong is closed for monsoon

activities.name: Maenam Beach — Quiet Beach Day
activities.description: Escape the crowds at Maenam, a long stretch of golden sand on the north coast with a laid-back village feel. The beach is lined with simple restaurants and beach bars rather than resorts. The Pralarn pier here is the launch point for ferries to Koh Phangan and Koh Tao.
tips: Best quiet beach on the island — popular with long-stay travelers and families
Sunset views from Maenam are excellent facing north-northwest

activities.name: Choeng Mon Beach
activities.description: A secluded crescent-shaped bay on Samui's northeast tip, favored by luxury resorts and families seeking peace. The shallow, calm waters are ideal for children, and the small village has just enough restaurants without feeling touristy. Arguably Samui's most beautiful beach proportionally.
tips: One of the calmest swimming beaches year-round due to its sheltered position
Several luxury resorts here allow day visitors to use their pools with a minimum spend

activities.name: ATV / Jungle Safari Tour
activities.description: Ride an ATV through Samui's jungle interior, passing rubber plantations, coconut groves, and hilltop viewpoints. Most safari tours combine ATV riding with visits to the Secret Buddha Garden, Na Muang Waterfalls, and a panoramic viewpoint. A muddy, adrenaline-fueled way to see the island's interior.
tips: Wear clothes you don't mind getting dirty — you will get muddy
Choose operators that include helmets and proper safety briefings
Combine with Secret Buddha Garden for the best value
